title: Cleretum rourkei
intro: Cleretum rourkei, also known as the vygie or succulent vygie, is a species of succulent plant in the family Aizoaceae. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions, such as wound healing and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Cleretum rourkei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cleretum rourkei is often prepared as a tea or tincture, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments, to harness its purported health benefits.
taxonomy: Cleretum rourkei is a member of the plant family Aizoaceae, commonly known as the ice plant family. This family consists of succulent plants, primarily found in Africa, and includes species such as succulents, mesembs, and stone plants. Within the Aizoaceae family, Cleretum rourkei is part of the tribe Ruschieae, which comprises many small-flowered, yellow or pink-coloured plants.
